confirmed_findings: release-cut.ps1 unconditionally passed Preview/PreviewOutputPath/PruneBundledExtras into sync-bundled-vibe.ps1, which breaks legacy or slimmer sync contracts
config/wave64-82-planning-board.json shipped question-mark corruption in every primary_goal entry
config/wave83-100-gate-manifest.json validated references/release-evidence-bundle-contract.md without declaring it in required_assets
release/governance docs still contained duplicate wording, stale updated_at metadata, or placeholder release-note references
rejected_findings: The docs/frontmatter-bom-governance.md style path references in wave boards are deliberate compatibility entrypoints, not broken paths
The hard-coded runtime-session path in the frozen v3.0.2 requirement/plan artifacts was left intact because those docs are run-specific evidence, not reusable templates
The Agent-S display-name wording was left intact because the repository still intentionally mixes upstream display names and canonical slugs in human-facing governance text and keyword checks
outcome_summary: Added a regression test that proves release-cut must not leak undeclared sync arguments into legacy sync-bundled-vibe contracts, watched it fail, then repaired the script with parameter guards.
Restored readable UTF-8 planning-board goals for Wave64-82 and repaired the wave83-100 release-evidence manifest declaration.
Cleaned up release/governance evidence surfaces only where the review findings were concrete and verification-backed.
